target.name: The Graph
notes: Rewards are in GRT on Arbitrum and denominated in USD.
PoC is required for all severities.
KYC is required for payout processing.
The Immunefi page reports 35 total smart-contract assets in scope; this v1 file captures the first verified high-priority page entries and should be refreshed before submission work.

hard_guardrails: Use local repo analysis, local tests, or local forks only.
Do not test against public mainnet or public testnet deployments.
Do not build phishing, social engineering, DoS, or exploit automation against real systems.
prohibited_activities: Any testing on mainnet or public testnet deployed code; all testing should be done on local forks of either public testnet or mainnet.
Any testing with pricing oracles or third-party smart contracts.
Attempting phishing or other social engineering attacks against employees or customers.
Any testing with third-party systems and applications.
Any denial of service attacks executed against project assets.
Automated testing of services that generates significant amounts of traffic.
Public disclosure of an unpatched vulnerability in an embargoed bounty.
Any other actions prohibited by Immunefi Rules.
out_of_scope: Frontrunning, including back running and sandwich attacks.
Known issues previously reported in security audits.
Natural network activity like curation whose mechanisms could result in unprofitable actions for a stakeholder.
Attacks that rely on social engineering.
Attacks requiring access to a victim machine.
Attacks of third-party service providers.
Best practice critiques.
Indexer port configurations not aligned with best practices.
Attacks that have the potential to impact token price.
Testnet assets.
Man in the middle attacks.
Incorrect data supplied by third-party oracles, except oracle manipulation or flash loan attacks are not automatically excluded.
Impacts requiring basic economic and governance attacks, such as 51% attacks.
Lack of liquidity impacts.
Impacts from Sybil attacks.
Impacts involving centralization risks.
Impacts caused by attacks requiring leaked keys or credentials.
Impacts caused by attacks requiring privileged addresses without additional privilege modification.
Mentions of secrets in GitHub without proof they are in use in production.
Feature requests.
Impacts on test files and configuration files unless stated otherwise.
reporting_requirements: Submit vulnerabilities to The Graph through Immunefi first.
Include reproducible steps, relevant links, docs, code, and evidence.
Submit one vulnerability per report; if the same bug affects multiple in-scope assets, mention them in one report.
Do not publicly disclose before resolution.
Inline PoC scripts are required for smart contract bugs and should not require external downloads.

impacts_in_scope.title: A bug that could cause significant (>$1M) user funds to be lost or stolen directly from protocol smart contracts, not including slashing.
An economic attack other than a basic 51% governance attack that could cause significant (>$1M) user funds to be lost or stolen directly from protocol smart contracts.
A bug that could cause network participants to be impersonated and unwanted actions being taken, such as user funds being stolen directly from protocol smart contracts.

assets_in_scope.name: GraphTokenLockWallet
assets_in_scope.notes: Token lock wallet for distribution flows.

assets_in_scope.name: RewardsManager
assets_in_scope.target: Not displayed in first scope page

assets_in_scope.name: L1Staking
assets_in_scope.notes: Likely priority because rewards mention Indexer and Delegator funds.

assets_in_scope.name: L1GNS
assets_in_scope.notes: Graph Name Service / subgraph ownership style logic.

assets_in_scope.name: DisputeManager
assets_in_scope.notes: Dispute and slashing-adjacent logic; slashing itself is excluded from critical funds-loss impact wording.

assets_in_scope.name: Curation
assets_in_scope.notes: Natural unprofitable curation activity is out of scope; focus on direct fund loss bugs.

assets_in_scope.name: BillingConnector
assets_in_scope.notes: Connector logic often has trust-boundary and accounting assumptions.

assets_in_scope.name: Governor
assets_in_scope.notes: Basic 51% governance attacks are out of scope; focus only on implementation bugs.

assets_in_scope.name: BridgeEscrow
assets_in_scope.notes: Escrowed funds plus bridge assumptions make this high priority.

audits.notes: Known audit issues are out of scope.
Known audit issues are out of scope.
Known audit issues are out of scope.
Issuance changes may include upgrades not yet deployed; verify asset is listed in scope before testing.

resources.description: A list of all security audits performed since 2020.

resources.title: Security Audit: Protocol Issuance Changes
resources.description: Audits related to issuance changes; some may cover upgrades not yet deployed and therefore ineligible until listed in scope.

resources.description: Audits related to the Horizon upgrade since 2024.

resources.title: Smart Contracts
resources.description: Each smart contract in scope has its own README.md.
